text: The gallbladder fundus is retracted towards the right diaphragm using the assistant port. The surgeon utilizes a robotic hook in arm one and fenestrated bipolar forceps in arm two to perform dissection. The hook acts as a versatile instrument, enabling anterior and posterior dissection, creating a window behind the cystic duct. The critical view of safety is achieved, revealing the cystic artery, cystic duct, and liver surface. Hem-o-lok clips are applied to the duct and artery for division, aided by the three-dimensional high-definition stereoscopic vision.

The surgeon uses routine laparoscopic scissors to divide the cystic duct and artery. The gallbladder is then dissected off and placed into an endobag. The endobag is retrieved through the umbilical trocar after the robot is undocked, and a 5 mm scope is inserted through the left hypochondrial trocar for visualization.
